format_version.description: Optional (SHOULD, df-000): payload-shape semver (MAJOR.MINOR.PATCH) signalling which spec generation produced this output. Absent on older producers; a client MUST treat absence as a pre-format_version baseline shape and MUST NOT infer the shape from protocol_version (the pinned MCP protocol version, orthogonal to payload evolution). Independent of the spec document's version.

doc_counts_available.description: When false, doc_counts and total_docs are zero-valued and must not be interpreted as an empty corpus.

watch_overflows.description: Optional (§15.6, dir2mcp #591): count of fsnotify kernel event-buffer overflows over the watcher's lifetime. Non-zero means some filesystem changes were reconciled by a periodic rescan rather than per-event. Absent when not running the watcher / unsupported.

stt_provider.description: STT provider name; not a closed enum (any STT-capable provider per SPEC §8.2).

recent_failures.description: Optional. Up to recent_failures_limit (default 20) of the most-recent documents with status='error', newest first by mtime_unix. Implementations MAY omit when no failures are recorded; clients MUST treat omission as 'no recent failures', not as 'unsupported'.

error_message.description: Short, single-line, length-capped (SHOULD cap at 512 bytes on a UTF-8 rune boundary) explanation of why this document failed ingest. Control characters MUST be stripped. Never contains secrets or raw file content.

skip_reasons.description: Optional honest-coverage breakdown: one {reason, count} entry per distinct reason a document was set to status='skipped' during ingest, counted across the current corpus (parallel to recent_failures). Unlike doc_counts (which groups status='ready' docs and overstates coverage), this reports what was NOT indexed and why. Implementations MAY omit when nothing was skipped; clients MUST treat omission as 'nothing skipped', not as 'unsupported'. Zero-count reasons are omitted; an empty corpus omits the field.

reason.description: Stable, closed skip-reason enum for a given spec minor. New reasons are added only by a minor version bump (additive); a client MAY receive an unrecognized value from a newer server and SHOULD render it verbatim rather than error.

count.description: Number of documents skipped for this reason in the current corpus. Always >= 1 (zero-count reasons are omitted).
